title: Narada proper hosted telemetry and Site Registry separation outcome
summary: Narada proper understood narada-andrey local relation admission and separated the hosted Site telemetry publication path from the hosted Site Registry relation path. The chapter range 1475-1481 closes with dry-run registry planning present locally and live registry publication still pending on an admitted registry-owner capability and relation admin credential binding.
message: site-telemetry publish is not the relation publication path. It remains the telemetry signal publication family, not hosted registry relation activation.

The Site Registry relation family now has a dry-run planner command locally: narada site-registry relation plan-transition. It validates payload shape and evidence posture without live network or secret resolution.

Live relation publication remains pending. It must not be represented as complete until a registry-owner crossing, relation admin credential/capability binding, and guarded live narada site-registry relation publish-transition --live --payload-file <file> task exist.

Task 1480 deferred live publication because capability list returned zero grants, credential preflight for config-ref:NARADA_SITE_REGISTRY_RELATION_ADMIN_TOKEN reported local_env_status=missing with raw_secret_exposed=false, and task 1479 was still claimed/unreported at inspection time.
No raw secret values, Cloudflare D1/KV mutation, live relation transition, or registry completion claim was performed.

mcp_routed_submission.reason: Cross-Site MCP mutation is not admitted in v1 fabric proof; canonical_inbox_cross_site_submission capability missing.

mcp_explicit_root_submission.reason: Cross-Site MCP mutation is not admitted in v1 fabric proof.

delivery_context.fallback: Submit through the target Site authority surface using narada inbox submit --cwd C:\Users\Andrey\Narada.

recommendation: Treat this as a bounded status notification, not as target local admission evidence or hosted registry completion.
